excerpt: Do not befriend the drunkard, for he is cursed, and he who sells wine... They are called enemies of God in heaven.
source_location_hint: Section on Prophet's Counsels to ʿAlī

excerpt: Recite also this prayer, which Gabriel revealed to me: O God, I beseech Thee for pardon and well-being...
When God created Paradise, He asked it, 'For whom have I fashioned you?' Paradise replied, 'For every generous soul and one who fears Thee.'
source_work: Traditional Islamic literature on Divine creation

excerpt: Perform ablution with care before sleep, for it is a foundation of faith... God will reward each such ablution as if you had worshipped for fifty years.

On the Day of Resurrection, a creature called Ḥārith... shall seek five kinds of humans: Those who do not pray, those who do not pay zakat, disobedient parents, alcoholics, and those who speak of worldly matters in the mosque.
source_location_hint: Section on creatures of the Day of Resurrection

excerpt: A believer is not one whose outward gathering is pure alone; a believer is one whose solitude is pure.
Shall I not inform you of the finest treasure? Listen well: she is a virtuous woman.
We are departing from the lesser struggle (jihād al-asghar) to the greater struggle (jihād al-akbar). Your greatest enemy is the self dwelling between your two sides.
A disbelieving neighbor has one share of rights; a Muslim neighbor has double; a relative neighbor has threefold.
The believers are brothers one to another, so make peace between your brothers, and fear God, that mercy may be shown to you.
Let not a people laugh at another people... nor let women laugh at women... nor reproach one another, nor call one another by bad names.
A painful punishment is prepared for them.
O you who believe, beware of suspicion, for suspicion in some cases is a sin. And spy not, nor backbite one another. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother?
source_location_hint: Verse on suspicion, spying, and backbiting

excerpt: The Messenger of God said, 'The good deeds of the backbiter are transferred from his record into the record of him who is backbitten.'

I am preoccupied with the faults of my own soul and thus distracted from the faults of others. Whoever is preoccupied with knowing the faults of his own soul becomes distracted from seeking faults in the souls of others.
source_location_hint: Section on finding one's own faults

excerpt: Take not God's commandments as mockery. Remember the favors God has bestowed upon you... Beware of disobedience to God.
